[https://books.google.com/books?id=tVv6OAAACAAJ ''Jinn┼Ź Sh┼Źt┼Źki: A Chronicle of Gods and Sovereigns''.] New York: Columbia University Press. {{ISBN|978-0-231-04940-5}}; {{OCLC|59145842}} {{Refend}} == External links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20100528074836/http://www.myj7000.jp-biz.net/clan/02/021/02140.htm ŠŚąŠťČŃü«ŔőŚňşŚ7000ňéĹ ňžôŠ░ĆÚí×ňłąňĄžŔŽ│ ňĄžňÇşňŤŻÚÇá] {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}} == References == * Tsutomu Ujitani, "Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1)," Kodansha Academic Library, 1992 * Takao Takaga, "Compilation of Ancient Clan Genealogies," Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986 ]]}} {{translated page|ja|ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│|version=98569775|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Yamato no Iotari]] [[simple:Immanuelle/Yamato no Iotari]] [[sn:Yamato no Iotari]] [[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Iotari]] [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]] [[sn:amato no Iotari]] [[ja:Yamato no Iotari]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:8th-century deaths]] [[Category:Yamato clan]] [[Category:ňąłŔë»ŠÖéń╗úŃü«Ŕ▓┤ŠŚĆ]] [[Category:ňżôń║öńŻŹńŞŐňĆŚńŻŹŔÇů]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamato no Iotari]][[Category:qq]]

{{infobox royalty|Š░ĆňÉŹ=ňĄžňÇş ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|image=|imageŃéÁŃéĄŃé║=|imageŔ¬ČŠśÄ=|ŠÖéń╗ú=[[ÚúŤÚ│ąŠÖéń╗ú]] - [[ňąłŔë»ŠÖéń╗ú]]|šöčŔ¬Ľ=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šş╗Š▓í=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šö╣ňÉŹ=|alternative names=|ŔźíňĆĚ=|šą×ňĆĚ=|ŠłĺňÉŹ=|ňóôŠëÇ=|ň«śńŻŹ=[[ňżôń║öńŻŹ|ňżôń║öńŻŹńŞŐ]]Ńâ╗[[ňłĹÚâĘšťü|ňłĹÚâĘň░ĹŔ╝ö]]|ńŞ╗ňÉŤ=[[ŠľçŠşŽňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠśÄňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠşúňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[:en:Emperor Sh┼Źmu|Emperor Sh┼Źmu]]|Š░ĆŠŚĆ=ňĄžňÇş[[ň┐îň»Ş]]´╝ł[[ňÇşňŤŻÚÇá]]´╝ë|šłÂŠ»Ź=|ňůäň╝č=|ňŽ╗=|ňşÉ=|šë╣ŔĘśń║őÚáů=|image=Ohyamato-jinja Haiden.jpg|image caption=Oyamato Shrine, their main shrine|caption=[[┼îyamato Shrine]], the main shrine of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]|province of origin=[[Yamato Province]]|home province=[[Yamato Province]]|founder={{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}},
{{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[Nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[Mount Miwa]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ŃÇÄ[[šÂÜŠŚąŠťČš┤Ç]]ŃÇĆ[[:en:šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓|šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓]]3rd year 10Šťł29ŠŚąŠŁí == Family tree == Many clans may be descendants of Furutama no Mikoto. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} == See also == * '''{{ill|Yamato no Tehiko|ja|ňÇşŠëőňŻŽ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Tehiko|fr|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Tehiko|lt1=Yamato no Tehiko|lt2=ŠëőňŻŽ|complete=true|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Tehiko|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Yamato no Iotari|ja|ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Iotari|fr|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Iotari|lt1=Yamato no Iotari|lt2=ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|complete=true|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Iotari|qq=}}''' * {{ill|Yamato no Agoko|ja|ňÇşňÉżňşÉš▒á|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Agoko|fr|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Agoko|lt1=Yamogokoro no Sukune|lt2=ňÇşňÉżňşÉš▒áň«┐šąó|complete=true|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Agoko|qq=}} * {{ill|┼îkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)|lt=┼îkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)|lt=Yamato Okunitama Shrine|ja|ňĄžňĺîňĄžňŤŻÚşéšą×šĄż|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine|qq=}} * [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] * {{ill|Princess Yamato Totohi Momoso|en|fr|Yamatototohimomoso-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Yamato Totohi Momoso|qq=}} == footnote == == Notes == {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist}} Trubner (for the Japan Society of London)|year=1896|isbn=9780524053478|volume=1|place=London|oclc=448337491|author-link=William George Aston}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ (JHTI)|publisher=UC Berkeley}}, searchtext resource to retrieve kanbun text vs. English tr. (Aston) in blocs. *{{cite book|title=Nihon shoki (Nihon Shoki)|last=Ujiya|first=Tsutomu (ň«çŠ▓╗Ŕ░Ěňşč)|publisher=Kodansha|year=1988|isbn=978-0-8021-5058-5|volume=ńŞŐ}}, modern Japanese translation. *{{cite book|title=The Kojiki|last=Chamberlain|first=Basil Hall|publisher=Kadokawa|year=1919|oclc=1882339|author-link=Basil Hall Chamberlain}} [http://www.sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/index.htm sacred texts] *{{cite book|title=Shintei Kojiki (Šľ░ŔĘé ňĆĄń║őŔĘś)|last=Takeda|first=Yukichi (ŠşŽšö░šąÉňÉë)|publisher=Kadokawa|year=1977|isbn=4-04-400101-4}}, annotated Japanese. ====Secondary sources==== * [[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichir┼Ź Ishida, eds. (1979). [https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Gukansho ''Gukansh┼Ź: The Future and the Past''.] Berkeley: University of California Press. {{ISBN|978-0-520-03460-0}}; {{OCLC|251325323}} * [[Louis-Fr├ęd├ęric|Nussbaum, Louis-Fr├ęd├ęric]] and K├Ąthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia''.] Cambridge: [[:en:Harvard University Press|Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; {{OCLC|58053128}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[Nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[Mount Miwa]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ŃÇÄ[[šÂÜŠŚąŠťČš┤Ç]]ŃÇĆ[[:en:šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓|šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓]]3rd year 10Šťł29ŠŚąŠŁí == Family tree == Many clans may be descendants of Furutama no Mikoto. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ŃÇÄ[[šÂÜŠŚąŠťČš┤Ç]]ŃÇĆ[[:en:šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓|šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓]]3rd year 10Šťł29ŠŚąŠŁí == Family tree == Many clans may be descendants of Furutama no Mikoto. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[Nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[Mount Miwa]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[Nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. {{infobox royalty|Š░ĆňÉŹ=ňĄžňÇş ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|image=|imageŃéÁŃéĄŃé║=|imageŔ¬ČŠśÄ=|ŠÖéń╗ú=[[ÚúŤÚ│ąŠÖéń╗ú]] - [[ňąłŔë»ŠÖéń╗ú]]|šöčŔ¬Ľ=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šş╗Š▓í=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šö╣ňÉŹ=|alternative names=|ŔźíňĆĚ=|šą×ňĆĚ=|ŠłĺňÉŹ=|ňóôŠëÇ=|ň«śńŻŹ=[[ňżôń║öńŻŹ|ňżôń║öńŻŹńŞŐ]]Ńâ╗[[ňłĹÚâĘšťü|ňłĹÚâĘň░ĹŔ╝ö]]|ńŞ╗ňÉŤ=[[ŠľçŠşŽňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠśÄňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠşúňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[:en:Emperor Sh┼Źmu|Emperor Sh┼Źmu]]|Š░ĆŠŚĆ=ňĄžňÇş[[ň┐îň»Ş]]´╝ł[[ňÇşňŤŻÚÇá]]´╝ë|šłÂŠ»Ź=|ňůäň╝č=|ňŽ╗=|ňşÉ=|šë╣ŔĘśń║őÚáů=|image=Ohyamato-jinja Haiden.jpg|image caption=Oyamato Shrine, their main shrine|caption=[[┼îyamato Shrine]], the main shrine of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]|province of origin=[[Yamato Province]]|home province=[[Yamato Province]]|founder={{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}},
{{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[Nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ŃÇÄ[[šÂÜŠŚąŠťČš┤Ç]]ŃÇĆ[[:en:šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓|šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓]]3rd year 10Šťł29ŠŚąŠŁí == Family tree == Many clans may be descendants of Furutama no Mikoto. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} == See also == * '''{{ill|Yamato no Tehiko|ja|ňÇşŠëőňŻŽ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Tehiko|fr|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Tehiko|lt1=Yamato no Tehiko|lt2=ŠëőňŻŽ|complete=true|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Tehiko|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Yamato no Iotari|ja|ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Iotari|fr|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Iotari|lt1=Yamato no Iotari|lt2=ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|complete=true|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Iotari|qq=}}''' * {{ill|Yamato no Agoko|ja|ňÇşňÉżňşÉš▒á|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Agoko|fr|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Agoko|lt1=Yamogokoro no Sukune|lt2=ňÇşňÉżňşÉš▒áň«┐šąó|complete=true|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Agoko|qq=}} * {{ill|┼îkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)|lt=┼îkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)|lt=Yamato Okunitama Shrine|ja|ňĄžňĺîňĄžňŤŻÚşéšą×šĄż|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine|qq=}} * [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] * {{ill|Princess Yamato Totohi Momoso|en|fr|Yamatototohimomoso-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Yamato Totohi Momoso|qq=}} == footnote == == Notes == {{notelist}} === References === {{reflist}} (JHTI)|publisher=UC Berkeley}}, searchtext resource to retrieve kanbun text vs. English tr. (Aston) in blocs. *{{cite book|title=Nihon shoki (Nihon Shoki)|last=Ujiya|first=Tsutomu (ň«çŠ▓╗Ŕ░Ěňşč)|publisher=Kodansha|year=1988|isbn=978-0-8021-5058-5|volume=ńŞŐ}}, modern Japanese translation. *{{cite book|title=The Kojiki|last=Chamberlain|first=Basil Hall|publisher=Kadokawa|year=1919|oclc=1882339|author-link=Basil Hall Chamberlain}} [http://www.sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/index.htm sacred texts] *{{cite book|title=Shintei Kojiki (Šľ░ŔĘé ňĆĄń║őŔĘś)|last=Takeda|first=Yukichi (ŠşŽšö░šąÉňÉë)|publisher=Kadokawa|year=1977|isbn=4-04-400101-4}}, annotated Japanese. ====Secondary sources==== * [[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichir┼Ź Ishida, eds. (1979). [https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Gukansho ''Gukansh┼Ź: The Future and the Past''.] Berkeley: University of California Press. {{ISBN|978-0-520-03460-0}}; {{OCLC|251325323}} * [[:en:Louis Fr├ęd├ęric|Nussbaum, Louis-Fr├ęd├ęric]] and K├Ąthe Roth. [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia''.] Cambridge: [[:en:Harvard University Press|Harvard University Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; {{OCLC|58053128}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[Nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[:en:Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ŃÇÄ[[šÂÜŠŚąŠťČš┤Ç]]ŃÇĆ[[:en:šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓|šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓]]3rd year 10Šťł29ŠŚąŠŁí == Family tree == Many clans may be descendants of Furutama no Mikoto. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[:en:Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] in [[Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor {{infobox royalty|Š░ĆňÉŹ=ňĄžňÇş ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|image=|imageŃéÁŃéĄŃé║=|imageŔ¬ČŠśÄ=|ŠÖéń╗ú=[[ÚúŤÚ│ąŠÖéń╗ú]] - [[ňąłŔë»ŠÖéń╗ú]]|šöčŔ¬Ľ=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šş╗Š▓í=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šö╣ňÉŹ=|alternative names=|ŔźíňĆĚ=|šą×ňĆĚ=|ŠłĺňÉŹ=|ňóôŠëÇ=|ň«śńŻŹ=[[ňżôń║öńŻŹ|ňżôń║öńŻŹńŞŐ]]Ńâ╗[[ňłĹÚâĘšťü|ňłĹÚâĘň░ĹŔ╝ö]]|ńŞ╗ňÉŤ=[[ŠľçŠşŽňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠśÄňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠşúňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[:en:Emperor Sh┼Źmu|Emperor Sh┼Źmu]]|Š░ĆŠŚĆ=ňĄžňÇş[[ň┐îň»Ş]]´╝ł[[ňÇşňŤŻÚÇá]]´╝ë|šłÂŠ»Ź=|ňůäň╝č=|ňŽ╗=|ňşÉ=|šë╣ŔĘśń║őÚáů=|image=Ohyamato-jinja Haiden.jpg|image caption=Oyamato Shrine, their main shrine|caption=[[┼îyamato Shrine]], the main shrine of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]|province of origin=[[Yamato Province]]|home province=[[Yamato Province]]|founder={{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}},
{{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[:en:Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[:en:Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. {{infobox royalty|Š░ĆňÉŹ=ňĄžňÇş ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|image=|imageŃéÁŃéĄŃé║=|imageŔ¬ČŠśÄ=|ŠÖéń╗ú=[[ÚúŤÚ│ąŠÖéń╗ú]] - [[ňąłŔë»ŠÖéń╗ú]]|šöčŔ¬Ľ=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šş╗Š▓í=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šö╣ňÉŹ=|alternative names=|ŔźíňĆĚ=|šą×ňĆĚ=|ŠłĺňÉŹ=|ňóôŠëÇ=|ň«śńŻŹ=[[ňżôń║öńŻŹ|ňżôń║öńŻŹńŞŐ]]Ńâ╗[[ňłĹÚâĘšťü|ňłĹÚâĘň░ĹŔ╝ö]]|ńŞ╗ňÉŤ=[[ŠľçŠşŽňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠśÄňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠşúňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[:en:Emperor Sh┼Źmu|Emperor Sh┼Źmu]]|Š░ĆŠŚĆ=ňĄžňÇş[[ň┐îň»Ş]]´╝ł[[ňÇşňŤŻÚÇá]]´╝ë|šłÂŠ»Ź=|ňůäň╝č=|ňŽ╗=|ňşÉ=|šë╣ŔĘśń║őÚáů=|image=Ohyamato-jinja Haiden.jpg|image caption=Oyamato Shrine, their main shrine|caption=[[┼îyamato Shrine]], the main shrine of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]|province of origin=[[Yamato Province]]|home province=[[Yamato Province]]|founder={{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}},
{{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[:en:Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[:en:Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ŃÇÄ[[šÂÜŠŚąŠťČš┤Ç]]ŃÇĆ[[:en:šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓|šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓]]3rd year 10Šťł29ŠŚąŠŁí == Family tree == Many clans may be descendants of Furutama no Mikoto. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} Tehiko|lt2=ŠëőňŻŽ|complete=true|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Tehiko|qq=}}''' * '''{{ill|Yamato no Iotari|ja|ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Sukune|lt2=ňÇşňÉżňşÉš▒áň«┐šąó|complete=true|en:draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Agoko|qq=}} * {{ill|┼îkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)|lt=┼îkunitama Shrine
