[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[:en:Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[:en:Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[:en:Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[:en:Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[:en:Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[:en:Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[:en:Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[:en:Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[:en:Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=ňĄęŠşŽňĄęšÜç|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[:en:Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=ňĄęŠşŽňĄęšÜç|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[:en:Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[:en:Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[:en:Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[:en:Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Ry┼źmaro|WD=Q134862753}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432454]] Wikidata name: Yamato no Iotari [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox royalty|Š░ĆňÉŹ=ňĄžňÇş ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|image=|imageŃéÁŃéĄŃé║=|imageŔ¬ČŠśÄ=|ŠÖéń╗ú=[[ÚúŤÚ│ąŠÖéń╗ú]] - [[ňąłŔë»ŠÖéń╗ú]]|šöčŔ¬Ľ=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šş╗Š▓í=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šö╣ňÉŹ=|alternative names=|ŔźíňĆĚ=|šą×ňĆĚ=|ŠłĺňÉŹ=|ňóôŠëÇ=|ň«śńŻŹ=[[ňżôń║öńŻŹ|ňżôń║öńŻŹńŞŐ]]Ńâ╗[[ňłĹÚâĘšťü|ňłĹÚâĘň░ĹŔ╝ö]]|ńŞ╗ňÉŤ=[[ŠľçŠşŽňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠśÄňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠşúňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[:en:Emperor Sh┼Źmu|Emperor Sh┼Źmu]]|Š░ĆŠŚĆ=ňĄžňÇş[[ň┐îň»Ş]]´╝ł[[ňÇşňŤŻÚÇá]]´╝ë|šłÂŠ»Ź=|ňůäň╝č=|ňŽ╗=|ňşÉ=|šë╣ŔĘśń║őÚáů=|image=Ohyamato-jinja Haiden.jpg|image caption=Oyamato Shrine, their main shrine|caption=[[┼îyamato Shrine]], the main shrine of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]|province of origin=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|home province=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|founder={{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}},
{{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=ňĄęŠşŽňĄęšÜç|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[:en:Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[:en:Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[:en:Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[:en:Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ŃÇÄ[[šÂÜŠŚąŠťČš┤Ç]]ŃÇĆ[[:en:šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓|šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓]]3rd year 10Šťł29ŠŚąŠŁí == Family tree == Many clans may be descendants of Furutama no Mikoto. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Ry┼źmaro|WD=Q134862753}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432454]] Wikidata name: Yamato no Iotari [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox royalty|Š░ĆňÉŹ=ňĄžňÇş ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|image=|imageŃéÁŃéĄŃé║=|imageŔ¬ČŠśÄ=|ŠÖéń╗ú=[[ÚúŤÚ│ąŠÖéń╗ú]] - [[ňąłŔë»ŠÖéń╗ú]]|šöčŔ¬Ľ=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šş╗Š▓í=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šö╣ňÉŹ=|alternative names=|ŔźíňĆĚ=|šą×ňĆĚ=|ŠłĺňÉŹ=|ňóôŠëÇ=|ň«śńŻŹ=[[ňżôń║öńŻŹ|ňżôń║öńŻŹńŞŐ]]Ńâ╗[[ňłĹÚâĘšťü|ňłĹÚâĘň░ĹŔ╝ö]]|ńŞ╗ňÉŤ=[[ŠľçŠşŽňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠśÄňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠşúňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[:en:Emperor Sh┼Źmu|Emperor Sh┼Źmu]]|Š░ĆŠŚĆ=ňĄžňÇş[[ň┐îň»Ş]]´╝ł[[ňÇşňŤŻÚÇá]]´╝ë|šłÂŠ»Ź=|ňůäň╝č=|ňŽ╗=|ňşÉ=|šë╣ŔĘśń║őÚáů=|image=Ohyamato-jinja Haiden.jpg|image caption=Oyamato Shrine, their main shrine|caption=[[┼îyamato Shrine]], the main shrine of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]|province of origin=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|home province=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|founder={{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}},
{{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=ňĄęŠşŽňĄęšÜç|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from [[:en:Asuka period|the Asuka period]] to [[:en:Nara period|the Nara period]] . His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[:en:Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[:en:Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[:en:Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ŃÇÄ[[šÂÜŠŚąŠťČš┤Ç]]ŃÇĆ[[:en:šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓|šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓]]3rd year 10Šťł29ŠŚąŠŁí == Family tree == Many clans may be descendants of Furutama no Mikoto. This includes the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]] and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|official Rank]] was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], [[:en:Empress Gensh┼Ź|Empress Gensh┼Ź]], and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of [[:en:Yamato Okunitama|Yamato Okunitama]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] by [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). [[:en:710|In 710]], he was promoted from [[:en:Wad┼Ź (era)|Junior]] Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year [[:en:Y┼Źr┼Ź|of the Yoro era]] ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and 20 pieces [[:en:Textile|of cloth]] . In the third year [[:en:Jinki (era)|of the Jinki]] era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given [[:en:Hemp|Hemp]], and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara}}, identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}.{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). {{ill|710|en|710|WD=Q23518|lt=In 710}}, he was promoted from {{ill|Wad┼Ź (era)|en|Wad┼Ź (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Junior}} Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year {{ill|Y┼Źr┼Ź|en|Y┼Źr┼Ź|WD=Q1373061|lt=of the Yoro era}} ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of {{ill|Hemp|en|Hemp|WD=Q7150699|lt=Hemp}}, and 20 pieces {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=of cloth}} . In the third year {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=of the Jinki}} era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given {{ill|Hemp|en|Hemp|WD=Q7150699|lt=Hemp}}, and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ŃÇÄ[[šÂÜŠŚąŠťČš┤Ç]]ŃÇĆ[[:en:šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓|šą×ŔşĚŠÖ»ÚŤ▓]]3rd year 10Šťł29ŠŚąŠŁí == Family tree == Many clans may be descendants of Furutama no Mikoto. This includes the {{ill|Owari clan|en|Owari clan|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari clan}} and [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]. This is a subject of debate.{{Cite web|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160422190938/http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/amabe/amabe-k2.htm|access-date=2023-12-13|website=web.archive.org}} [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Ry┼źmaro|WD=Q134862753}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432454]] Wikidata name: Yamato no Iotari [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox royalty|Š░ĆňÉŹ=ňĄžňÇş ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|image=|imageŃéÁŃéĄŃé║=|imageŔ¬ČŠśÄ=|ŠÖéń╗ú=[[ÚúŤÚ│ąŠÖéń╗ú]] - [[ňąłŔë»ŠÖéń╗ú]]|šöčŔ¬Ľ=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šş╗Š▓í=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šö╣ňÉŹ=|alternative names=|ŔźíňĆĚ=|šą×ňĆĚ=|ŠłĺňÉŹ=|ňóôŠëÇ=|ň«śńŻŹ=[[ňżôń║öńŻŹ|ňżôń║öńŻŹńŞŐ]]Ńâ╗[[ňłĹÚâĘšťü|ňłĹÚâĘň░ĹŔ╝ö]]|ńŞ╗ňÉŤ=[[ŠľçŠşŽňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠśÄňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠşúňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ{{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Sh┼Źmu}}|Š░ĆŠŚĆ=ňĄžňÇş[[ň┐îň»Ş]]´╝ł[[ňÇşňŤŻÚÇá]]´╝ë|šłÂŠ»Ź=|ňůäň╝č=|ňŽ╗=|ňşÉ=|šë╣ŔĘśń║őÚáů=|image=Ohyamato-jinja Haiden.jpg|image caption=Oyamato Shrine, their main shrine|caption=[[┼îyamato Shrine]], the main shrine of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]|province of origin={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}|home province={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}|founder={{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}},
{{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=ňĄęŠşŽňĄęšÜç|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=the Asuka period}} to {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=the Nara period}} . His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=official Rank}} was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], {{ill|Empress Gensh┼Ź|en|Empress Gensh┼Ź|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Gensh┼Ź}}, and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato Okunitama}} of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato Okunitama}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}.{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|Sakurai, During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}.{{Cite Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). {{ill|710|en|710|WD=Q23518|lt=In 710}}, he was promoted from {{ill|Wad┼Ź (era)|en|Wad┼Ź (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Junior}} Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year {{ill|Y┼Źr┼Ź|en|Y┼Źr┼Ź|WD=Q1373061|lt=of the Yoro era}} ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of {{ill|Hemp|en|Hemp|WD=Q7150699|lt=Hemp}}, and 20 pieces {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=of cloth}} . In the third year {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=of the Jinki}} era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given {{ill|Hemp|en|Hemp|WD=Q7150699|lt=Hemp}}, and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice. ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=ňĄęŠşŽňĄęšÜç|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=the Asuka period}} to {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=the Nara period}} . His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=official Rank}} was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], {{ill|Empress Gensh┼Ź|en|Empress Gensh┼Ź|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Gensh┼Ź}}, and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] [[fr:Yamato no Iotari]][[ja:ňĄžňÇşń║öšÖżŔÂ│]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Ry┼źmaro|WD=Q134862753}}}} {{infobox royalty|Š░ĆňÉŹ=ňĄžňÇş ń║öšÖżŔÂ│|image=|imageŃéÁŃéĄŃé║=|imageŔ¬ČŠśÄ=|ŠÖéń╗ú=[[ÚúŤÚ│ąŠÖéń╗ú]] - [[ňąłŔë»ŠÖéń╗ú]]|šöčŔ¬Ľ=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šş╗Š▓í=ńŞŹŠśÄ|Šö╣ňÉŹ=|alternative names=|ŔźíňĆĚ=|šą×ňĆĚ=|ŠłĺňÉŹ=|ňóôŠëÇ=|ň«śńŻŹ=[[ňżôń║öńŻŹ|ňżôń║öńŻŹńŞŐ]]Ńâ╗[[ňłĹÚâĘšťü|ňłĹÚâĘň░ĹŔ╝ö]]|ńŞ╗ňÉŤ=[[ŠľçŠşŽňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠśÄňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ[[ňůâŠşúňĄęšÜç]]Ôćĺ{{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Sh┼Źmu}}|Š░ĆŠŚĆ=ňĄžňÇş[[ň┐îň»Ş]]´╝ł[[ňÇşňŤŻÚÇá]]´╝ë|šłÂŠ»Ź=|ňůäň╝č=|ňŽ╗=|ňşÉ=|šë╣ŔĘśń║őÚáů=|image=Ohyamato-jinja Haiden.jpg|image caption=Oyamato Shrine, their main shrine|caption=[[┼îyamato Shrine]], the main shrine of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]|province of origin={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}|home province={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}|founder={{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}},
{{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}|father='''{{ill|Ry┼źmaro|ja|ÚżŹÚ║╗ňĹé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ry┼źmaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=ňĄęŠşŽňĄęšÜç|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}'s bestowed Rengo surname)}} '''Yamato no Iotari''' was [[:en:Japanese nobility|an aristocrat]] from {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=the Asuka period}} to {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=the Nara period}} . His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} was {{ill|10=ja|11=ň┐îň»Ş|Ikisui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . His {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=official Rank}} was {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|ňżôń║öńŻŹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and his job was {{ill|10=ja|11=f|Ministry_of_Justice_(pre-modern_Japan)|en|lt=Junior Assistant Minister of Justice|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} . He served under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}}, [[:en:Empress Gemmei|Empress Gemmei]], {{ill|Empress Gensh┼Ź|en|Empress Gensh┼Ź|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Gensh┼Ź}}, and {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}}. == Origin == The [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] was a clan of {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}. [[ňĄ¬šö░ń║«]]ŃÇÄňžôŠ░Ćň«Âš│╗ňĄžŔż×ňůŞŃÇĆ[[:en:ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś|ŔžĺňĚŁŠŤŞň║Ś]]ŃÇü1963rd year The clan's ancestor was {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|simple|User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}}, who was a distinguished figure during {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} . ŃÇÄ[[Nihon Shoki]]ŃÇĆšą×ŠşŽŠ«ÁŃÇéŃÇÄ[[Šľ░Šĺ░ňžôŠ░ĆÚî▓]]ŃÇĆňĄžňĺîšą×ňłąŃüžŃü»ŃÇîšą×ščąŠ┤ąňŻŽňĹŻŃÇŹŃüĘŃüÖŃéőŃÇé {{Short description|Japanese noble family|qq=draft}} Their [[Ujigami]] or clan god is {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato Okunitama}} of [[┼îyamato Shrine]]{{Cite web|title=International Symposium "Perspectives on Japanese history and literature from ancient historical records"|url=https://www.waseda.jp/inst/sgu/news-en/2019/04/12/5171|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Top Global University Project: Waseda Goes Global|language=en}} Some scholars interpret the ''kami'' as being a variant or epithet of [[┼îmononushi]] who has much more widespread worship.{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199ÔÇô217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018|doi-access=free}}{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ|title=Shinto: A History|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=2017|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-062171-1|language=en}}{{rp|22}} There is a complex myth about the origins of modern worship of {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|WD=Q11387080|lt=Yamato Okunitama}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}.{{Cite web|last=D|first=John|date=2012-11-10|title=Teeuwen on Shinto|url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/11/10/teeuwen-on-shinto/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Green Shinto|language=en-GB}}https://www.japanpolicyforum.jp/pdf/2016/no35/DJweb_35_cul_02.pdfhttps://freidok.uni-freiburg.de/fedora/objects/freidok:4635/datastreams/FILE1/content{{Citation|title=Book V|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi:_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_V|work=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 1|access-date=2023-05-04}} [[File:Hibara-jinja,_torii_closeup.jpg|thumb|240x240px|Hibara Shrine, at the foot of {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara}}, identified as the place where the Yata-no-Kagami and the Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi were first enshrined after their removal from the imperial palace.]] During {{ill|Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu%27s Eastern Expedition|qq=}} {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} was given the position of governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}.{{Cite web|title=Friday: Kojiki (ŃÇîń╣×ÚúčŃÇŹŃüžŃü»Ńü¬ŃüĆŃÇÄňĆĄń║őŔĘśŃÇĆŃüžŃüÖŃâĘ´╝ü´╝ü) #26|url=https://japaneseexperts.net/972/|access-date=2023-05-04|website=Japanese Experts Net|language=en-US}} And Saonetsuhiko became their ancestor.{{Cite web|date=2023-10-26|title=Saonetsuhiko {{!}} ňťőňşŞÚÖóňĄžňşŞŃâçŃéŞŃé┐ŃâźŃâčŃâąŃâ╝ŃéŞŃéóŃâá|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231026183115/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159|archive-date=2023-10-26|access-date=2023-12-04}} After the divination, {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Saonetsuhiko|ja|ŠĄÄŠá╣Š┤ąňŻŽ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated Nunaki-iri-hime. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|ňŞéšú»ÚĽĚň░żňŞé|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko.{{Cite web|title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208|url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208|access-date=2023-10-24|website=en.wikisource.org|language=en}} == Biography == In the first year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Monmu|qq=}} ( 697 ), he traveled overland to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} as an envoy to welcome {{ill|10=ja|11=Šľ░šżůńŻ┐|the Silla envoys|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} (his Rank at the time was Shin Daiichi). {{ill|710|en|710|WD=Q23518|lt=In 710}}, he was promoted from {{ill|Wad┼Ź (era)|en|Wad┼Ź (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Junior}} Sixth Rank to Junior Fifth Rank . In 714, he became {{ill|10=ja|11=Š░ĆÚĽĚŔÇů|the head of the clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} and was in charge of religious services. In the 7th year {{ill|Y┼Źr┼Ź|en|Y┼Źr┼Ź|WD=Q1373061|lt=of the Yoro era}} ( 723 ), Kii, a man from {{ill|Sakyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyo|qq=}}, presented a white turtle, which was deemed an auspicious omen, and as the [[kuni no miyatsuko]] (Owa kuni no miyatsuko) of the country that had brought the turtle, he was bestowed with 10 pieces of silk, 100 pieces of {{ill|Hemp|en|Hemp|WD=Q7150699|lt=Hemp}}, and 20 pieces {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=of cloth}} . In the third year {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=of the Jinki}} era ( 726 ), he was promoted to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank. In 727, at a banquet celebrating the birth of {{ill|Emperor Sh┼Źmu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sh┼Źmu|qq=}} 's son, {{ill|10=ja|11=ňč║šÄő|Prince Motoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}}, anyone of fifth {{ill|List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|lt=Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|qq=}} or higher was given {{ill|Hemp|en|Hemp|WD=Q7150699|lt=Hemp}}, and especially the eldest son of a prominent family of fifth Rank or higher was given an additional ten bolts of silk. However, Gohyakutaku was specially added to this list along with {{ill|10=ja|11=Ŕ¬┐ŠĚíŠÁĚ|Chotankai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} because of his advanced age.{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tsuki no Omi - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/person/TSUKI%20no%20Omi.html|access-date=2024-04-09|website=www.japanesewiki.com}} Year of death unknown. His final official Rank was Junior Fifth Rank and Junior Assistant Minister of Justice.
